rows.notes: Centered page title clearly written across top; no amounts present and ruling lines do not align any numbers to this line.
Amounts align to first numeric line; pounds '21' and shillings '5' clear. Pence digit looks like a '6' (clear loop) though handwriting is slightly slanted.

rows.description: Copyhold Comm. on sale of New Estate
rows.notes: Numbers '20 0 0' are well aligned in the £ | s | d columns; shillings and pence clearly '00'.
Pounds '5' and shillings '6' align to the pound and shilling columns. Pence column is blank/zero and appears as '0'. Slight ambiguity in the desc. spelling but amounts are legible.

Pounds '15' is clear; the middle column reads as '1' shilling but the digit is slightly crowded against ruling line making 1 vs 11 a possible ambiguity — chosen '1' because 11 would violate visual spacing. Pence looks like zero.
Clear small values '2 2 0' neatly aligned in the three numeric columns; legible handwriting and column separation unambiguous.
Numbers read clearly as '3 0 0' with good alignment to the £ | s | d ruling; no ambiguity.
Pounds '2' and shillings '10' are clear. Pence appears as '9' (a small looped figure) and aligns with the pence column; handwriting is slightly condensed but digits are consistent with column edges.
Large amount '74 1 3' clearly written and well aligned; digits are unambiguous and within valid ranges.
Pounds '150' is clearly written and markedly larger; shillings and pence '00' are obvious. Well aligned to numeric columns.
Pounds '1' clear; shillings read as '14' (the '1' is narrow but the following '4' is distinct). Pence '8' visible as a small loop; alignment supports this reading.
Clear '5 0 0' entry with strong alignment; no ambiguity.
Small neat '2 0 0' wholly aligned in columns; handwriting legible.
Pounds '2' and shillings '10' clearly visible. The description is slightly cramped but the numeric columns are clear; pence appears zero.
Amount reads as '18 2 0' and aligns well; the '2' in shillings is small but consistent with ruling position.
Pounds '8' clear; shillings '13' and pence '2' readable though pence digit is small. Column alignment supports these values.
Numbers read '2 7 6' and fit the £|s|d columns. Handwritten '7' and '6' are clear; description is abbreviated but legible.

rows.description: Castle sale of Timber. Commr. on
rows.notes: Pounds '6' and shillings '14' appear in the correct columns. The pence digit looks like '2' but is small and slightly smudged; column alignment favored reading as '2' rather than other shapes.
Values '9 13 0' are clearly positioned; pence column shows zero. Description hyphenation is transcribed as seen.
Pounds and shillings '6 15' are legible and aligned. Pence column empty/zero. Handwriting crisp for this row.
Follow-on 'Do' entry (same heading as previous) with amounts '3 11 0' clearly in columns; '11' shillings legible.
Large clear amount '65 0 0' aligned to columns; digits are unambiguous.
Pounds '46' and shillings '18' distinct; pence '8' visible though slightly small. All digits respect column boundaries.
Pounds and shillings '8 4' align well. Pence appears to be '5' but the final digit has light ink and could be 3 or 5; chosen '5' based on loop formation and spacing in pence column.
Numbers '28 5 0' are clear and aligned; pence column blank/zero. Description spelling preserved as on page.
Pounds '50' and shillings '3' clear; pence '6' readable though small. Alignment consistent with numeric ruling lines.
Pounds '20' and shillings '16' visible and fall neatly in their columns. Pence shows as zero. Description abbreviated as on the page.
Values '12 13 2' align with the £|s|d lines; pence '2' is small but distinct. Surname 'Walsh' and remainder of description read clearly.
Pounds '14' and shillings '2' appear in columns. Pence is likely zero; the final small mark near the pence column is faint leading to moderate uncertainty on pence (interpreted as 0).
Bottom boxed total clearly written as '2183 7 0' aligned to the numeric columns. Very clear final tally; used as alignment reference for column boundaries.
